taskDescription: Arrange the course of the facial nerve (CN VII) from its origin to its terminal branches in the correct order.
paragraphs: The facial nerve nucleus lies in the lower pons; it emerges at the cerebellopontine angle alongside CN VIII.
It enters the internal acoustic meatus and travels through the facial canal in the petrous temporal bone.
At the geniculum, the geniculate ganglion gives off the greater petrosal nerve to the lacrimal gland.
The nerve exits the skull through the stylomastoid foramen.
It enters the posterior surface of the parotid gland and divides into five terminal branches: temporal, zygomatic, buccal, marginal mandibular, and cervical.
